:::: {.panel-tabset .nav-pills} ## Prison Population
::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}

Prison Population Trends

Sentence about change in admissions from 2010 to 2020.


``` ::: ::: ### Number of Releases ::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}

Sentence about change in releases from 2010 to 2020.


``` ::: ::: ### Year-End Population ::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}

Sentence about change in year-end population from 2010 to 2020.


``` ::: ::: ::: ::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}

Admission Types

Prison populations are shaped by two primary groups: new court commitments, or individuals newly incarcerated for a criminal offense, and parole returns and revocations, those who return to prison for failing to meet the conditions of their parole. In Kansas in 2020, people in prison for new court commitments accounted for 91% of the prison population, while parole returns and revocations accounted for 9%.


``` ::: ::: ::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}

Who's in Prison?

``` ::: ::: ::: {.panel-tabset} ### Race and Ethinicity ::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}

In 2020, White, non-Hispanic individuals made up the largest portion of people in prison for both new court commitments and parole returns and revocations.

``` ::: ::: ### Gender ::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}

In 2020, male individuals made up the largest portion of people in prison for both new court commitments and parole returns and revocations.

``` ::: ::: ### Age ::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}

In 2020, individuals aged 25-34 years made up the largest portion of people in prison for both new court commitments and parole returns and revocations.

``` ::: ::: ::: ::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}

Sentence about most common offense.


``` ::: ::: ::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}

Sentence Length

``` ::: :::

## Parole Eligibility
::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}

How is Parole Eligibility Determined?



``` ::: ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}

Parole-Eligible Prison Population

In 2020, there were 10 people were eligible for parole but still in prison for new court commitments and with original sentence lengths ranging from 1 to 25 years. This group made up 0% of the prison population.



``` ::: ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}

People who are Eligible for Parole

``` ::: ::: ::: {.panel-tabset} ### Race and Ethnicity ::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}

In 2020, White, non-Hispanic people made up the largest proportion of those eligible for parole yet still incarcerated, comprising 50% of the parole-eligible population serving time for new court commitments and with an original sentence length between 1 to 25 years.


``` ::: ::: ### Gender ::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}

In 2020, male people made up the largest proportion of those eligible for parole yet still incarcerated, comprising 100% of the parole-eligible population serving time for new court commitments and with an original sentence length between 1 to 25 years.


``` ::: ::: ### Age ::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}

In 2020, people who were between the ages of 55+ years made up the largest proportion of those eligible for parole yet still incarcerated, comprising 50% of the parole-eligible population serving time for new court commitments and with an original sentence length between 1 to 25 years.


``` ::: ::: ::: ::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}

Sentence Lengths

In 2020, the majority of the parole-eligible prison population serving sentences for new court commitments with sentence lengths ranging from 1 to 25 years were incarcerated with an original sentence length of 10 to 24.9 years, accounting for 70% of this group.



``` ::: ::: ::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}

Criminal Offenses

In 2020, the majority of the parole-eligible prison population serving sentences for new court commitments with sentence lengths ranging from 1 to 25 years were incarcerated for offenses related to rape or sexual assault, accounting for 50% of this group.


``` ::: ::: ::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}

Missing Data

This section will have information about missing parole eligibility data for the state once new NCRP data is released.
``` ::: :::
